name: First Intifada
summary: The First Intifada was a Palestinian uprising against Israeli rule in the occupied territories, usually dated from 9 December 1987 to the Oslo I signing on 13 September 1993. It combined mass demonstrations, strikes, boycotts, stone-throwing, Molotov cocktails, militant attacks, Israeli military/police repression, and substantial intra-Palestinian violence against alleged collaborators. The most defensible death-toll range is roughly 2,100-2,350 deaths, depending on cutoff dates and whether AP, IDF, B'Tselem, or official Israeli figures are used.

military_killed.notes: Israeli security-force personnel killed by Palestinians. Low/point uses the canonical Oslo cutoff, 9 Dec 1987-13 Sep 1993: 43 in the occupied territories plus 17 within the Green Line. High counts through 31 Dec 1993: 46 in the occupied territories plus 19 within the Green Line.

breakdown.cause: shooting/beating/tear gas/direct force by Israeli security forces in occupied territories
breakdown.victims_group: Palestinians in the occupied territories including East Jerusalem
breakdown.notes: B'Tselem gives 1,070 Palestinians killed by Israeli security forces in the occupied territories through 13 Sep 1993; adding 14 Sep-31 Dec 1993 gives 1,100. B'Tselem does not provide a clean civilian/combatant split for Palestinian fatalities in this table.

breakdown.cause: settler/Israeli civilian violence in occupied territories
breakdown.victims_group: Palestinians in the occupied territories including East Jerusalem
breakdown.notes: B'Tselem gives 54 Palestinians killed by Israeli civilians in the occupied territories through 13 Sep 1993; adding 14 Sep-31 Dec 1993 gives 62.

breakdown.cause: direct force by Israeli security forces within the Green Line
breakdown.notes: B'Tselem gives 17 through 13 Sep 1993; adding 14 Sep-31 Dec 1993 gives 21.

breakdown.cause: violence by Israeli civilians within the Green Line
breakdown.notes: B'Tselem gives 21 through 13 Sep 1993 and no additional deaths in this category through 31 Dec 1993.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: This bucket records Palestinians directly killed by Israeli security forces or Israeli civilians. The public B'Tselem first-intifada table does not separate Palestinian combatants from civilians, so these figures are direct-death totals rather than a verified civilians-only count.

deaths_from_actions.notes: Human Rights Watch and other sources document curfews, school/university closures, deportations, administrative detention, house demolitions, travel restrictions, and other collective punishments during the uprising, but I found no reliable separate estimate of indirect deaths from these measures distinct from direct killings.

sides.name: Palestinian uprising organizations, militants, and local death squads
members: Unified National Leadership of the Uprising
Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine
Democratic Front for the Liberation of Palestine

military_killed.notes: Palestinian combatants/militants killed are not separately identified in the main public first-intifada fatality tables. Palestinian fatalities killed by Israelis are reported as Palestinians killed, without a clean civilian/combatant split.

breakdown.notes: B'Tselem gives 100 Israeli civilians killed by Palestinians through 13 Sep 1993: 47 in the occupied territories and 53 within the Green Line. Counting through 31 Dec 1993 gives 114: 58 in the occupied territories and 56 within the Green Line.

breakdown.victims_group: Palestinians accused of collaboration with Israel
breakdown.notes: B'Tselem cites Associated Press for 771 and the IDF Spokesperson for 942 Palestinians killed by other Palestinians on suspicion of collaboration between 9 Dec 1987 and 30 Nov 1993.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: This combines Israeli civilians killed by Palestinians and Palestinians killed by other Palestinians as alleged collaborators. The alleged-collaborator figures are especially uncertain and politically sensitive.

totals.notes: Grand low uses the Oslo-cutoff direct bilateral deaths from B'Tselem plus AP's 771 alleged-collaborator killings: 1,162 Palestinians killed by Israelis + 160 Israelis killed by Palestinians + 771 alleged collaborators = 2,093. Grand high uses end-1993 B'Tselem bilateral deaths plus the IDF Spokesperson's 942 alleged-collaborator killings: 1,204 + 179 + 942 = 2,325. If the official Israeli total of about 200 is substituted for B'Tselem's 179, the high-end roll-up rises to about 2,346. Palestinian combatant deaths are not separately coded, so military totals include only Israeli security-force fatalities.

key_atrocities.name: Haram al-Sharif / Temple Mount killings
key_atrocities.notes: On 8 Oct 1990, Israeli Border Police/security forces shot dead Palestinians at Haram al-Sharif/Temple Mount. Later PHRIC/IPS summaries use 17 deaths; early accounts reported 19-21.

key_atrocities.name: Tel Aviv-Jerusalem Bus 405 attack
key_atrocities.victims_group: Israeli and foreign civilian bus passengers
key_atrocities.notes: On 6 Jul 1989, Abd al-Hadi Ghanim seized the steering wheel of Egged bus 405 and drove it into a ravine, killing 16 civilians and wounding 27.

key_atrocities.name: Killings of suspected Palestinian collaborators
key_atrocities.victims_group: Palestinians accused of collaboration with Israel
key_atrocities.notes: Associated Press estimated 771; the IDF Spokesperson estimated 942 Palestinians killed by other Palestinians on suspicion of collaboration from 9 Dec 1987 to 30 Nov 1993.

key_atrocities.name: Mehola Junction bombing
key_atrocities.notes: On 16 Apr 1993, a Hamas-claimed suicide car bombing killed one civilian plus the bomber and injured 7-9 people.

uncertainty_notes: The largest uncertainties are the cutoff date, the lack of a Palestinian combatant/civilian split in major fatality tables, and the range for Palestinians killed as alleged collaborators. B'Tselem gives precise direct-death tables by perpetrator and location, but not a full combatant-status coding for Palestinians. Indirect deaths from curfews, closures, deportations, detention, and house demolitions are documented as policies but not reliably quantified as a separate mortality category.

sources.title: Collaborators in the Occupied Territories: Human Rights Abuses and Violations
Human Rights Watch World Report 1989 - Israeli-Occupied Territories
The First Intifada, 1987-1993
sources.publisher: Interactive Encyclopedia of the Palestine Question / Institute for Palestine Studies

sources.title: Victims of 1989 attack on bus No. 405 cry out
